steps.title: Identify the phrase requiring punctuation
steps.content: After “material,” the text continues with “to craft baskets that are both beautiful and whimsical.” This is an infinitive phrase—a fragment containing a verbal (“to craft”) but no independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
In Standard English, a period marks a sentence boundary and requires a complete sentence (independent clause) on both sides. “To craft baskets...” is a fragment, eliminating (C). A semicolon joins two independent clauses, but “to craft baskets” is not a clause, eliminating (A). A colon typically introduces lists or elaborations that are independent clauses; infinitive phrases don't fit that pattern, eliminating (B). An em dash can introduce elaborations and explanations without requiring a complete sentence—it clarifies what the material is used for, showing its purpose.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
